title: [SEC-01] Add unified auth middleware for execution APIs
body: Scope: enforce auth on /v1/jobs/*, /v1/workers/*, /v1/interrupts/*. DoD: missing credential returns 401; invalid credential returns 401; valid credential passes baseline API tests.

title: [SEC-02] Implement API key authentication
body: Scope: support key_id, hashed secret storage, key active/disabled state, and rotation-friendly model. DoD: disabled key is rejected immediately with 401 and covered by tests.

title: [SEC-03] Implement JWT authentication with JWKS
body: Scope: validate iss/aud/exp and cache JWKS for runtime verification. DoD: expired token and wrong audience are rejected; JWKS cache behavior is observable.

title: [SEC-04] Introduce minimum RBAC model
body: Scope: define admin/operator/worker roles and route-level permission matrix. DoD: unauthorized role access returns 403 and matrix is covered by automated tests.

title: [SEC-05] Persist audit logs for control-plane operations
body: Scope: record actor, action, resource, result, request_id, and timestamp for run/resume/replay/cancel/reject. DoD: 100 percent of key control actions produce durable audit entries.

title: [SEC-06] Add security regression test suite
body: Scope: add tests for auth bypass, privilege escalation, request_id spoof handling, and replay resistance baseline. DoD: CI has a dedicated security job and it is required for merge.

title: [DB-01] Complete PostgreSQL RuntimeRepository parity
body: Scope: implement PostgreSQL RuntimeRepository to match SQLite contract and semantics. DoD: same runtime repository test suite passes on sqlite and postgres backends.

title: [DB-02] Harden lease/attempt/interrupt transaction logic on PostgreSQL
body: Scope: enforce transaction boundaries and conflict handling for dispatch and ownership flows. DoD: concurrency tests show no double-dispatch and no inconsistent lease ownership.

title: [DB-03] Introduce schema migration workflow
body: Scope: add schema versioning with forward migrations and documented rollback path. DoD: clean init and incremental upgrade are repeatable in CI and local environments.

title: [DB-04] Add backend selection and startup health checks
body: Scope: allow runtime backend selection via config and fail fast on invalid DSN or missing schema. DoD: startup emits actionable error and exits non-zero for bad backend config.

title: [DB-05] Publish PostgreSQL backup and restore runbook
body: Scope: document and script backup, restore, and validation path for runtime data. DoD: one full rehearsal (backup to restore to resume execution) is recorded.

title: [DB-06] Add PostgreSQL integration matrix in CI
body: Scope: run key runtime tests on both sqlite and postgres, including crash recovery scenarios. DoD: CI blocks merge when either backend matrix fails.

title: [SCH-01] Add retry policies for attempts
body: Scope: support fixed and exponential backoff with max retry count. DoD: failed attempts retry according to policy and retry history is queryable.

title: [SCH-02] Add attempt timeout policy
body: Scope: define execution timeout and terminal behavior on timeout. DoD: timed-out attempts transition deterministically to configured terminal state.

title: [SCH-03] Introduce dead-letter queue model and APIs
body: Scope: persist unrecoverable attempts and expose list/inspect/replay APIs. DoD: DLQ attempts are visible and replayable through control-plane APIs.

title: [SCH-04] Add priority-aware dispatch
body: Scope: support attempt priority and prefer high-priority dispatch under mixed queues. DoD: load test shows lower wait latency for high-priority attempts.

title: [SCH-05] Add worker and tenant rate limits
body: Scope: implement configurable worker and tenant throttling integrated with backpressure decisions. DoD: over-limit traffic returns deterministic backpressure decisions and metrics.

title: [SCH-06] Build failover and conflict stress test suite
body: Scope: create fault-injection scripts for lease expiry, conflict, and failover recovery measurements. DoD: baseline report includes conflict rate, recovery latency, and throughput.

title: [OBS-01] Add end-to-end OpenTelemetry traces
body: Scope: instrument run/thread/attempt lifecycle with trace context propagation. DoD: one run can be traced end-to-end across API and worker flow.

title: [OBS-02] Expose Prometheus runtime metrics
body: Scope: expose queue depth, dispatch latency, lease conflict rate, and recovery latency metrics. DoD: /metrics endpoint is scrape-ready and metrics are documented.

title: [OBS-03] Provide Grafana dashboards and alert rules
body: Scope: add dashboard JSON and alert thresholds for error rate, recovery latency, and sustained backpressure. DoD: dashboard and alerts are validated against sample workload.

title: [REL-01] Add replay side-effect safety guard
body: Scope: persist effect log and idempotency fingerprint to avoid duplicated external side effects during replay. DoD: replay test proves no duplicate side-effect execution.

title: [OSS-01] Publish cargo-generate templates for service/worker/operator
body: Scope: package and document cargo-generate flow for the three starter archetypes. DoD: external user can scaffold and run each archetype with one command path.

title: [OSS-02] Complete production operations docs
body: Scope: publish deploy, upgrade, migration, SLO, and incident-response guides for operators. DoD: docs provide an end-to-end path from fresh deploy to production readiness.

title: [ARCH] Add async checkpoint API for better performance
body:
## Description

Currently the checkpoint API is synchronous, which blocks the runtime during persistence operations. Adding async checkpoint APIs would improve throughput.

## Scope
- Add async versions of checkpoint save/load functions
- Ensure backward compatibility
- Add tests for new async APIs

## Type
- type/feature
- area/runtime

title: [FEATURE] Add plugin system for custom node types
body:
## Description

Oris should support a plugin system that allows users to define custom node types at runtime.

## Scope
- Define plugin trait/interface
- Add registration mechanism for custom nodes
- Ensure type safety at runtime
- Add documentation and examples

## Motivation
Currently, node types are hardcoded. A plugin system would allow extensibility without modifying the core runtime.

## Type
- type/feature
- area/runtime

title: [CI-01] Consolidate CI workflows and enforce lint/example gates
body:
## Description

The repository currently runs two overlapping GitHub workflows (`.github/workflows/ci.yml` and `.github/workflows/rust.yml`). The stronger workflow still does not enforce `cargo clippy`, and critical examples are only validated manually.

## Scope
- Consolidate duplicate CI coverage into one canonical workflow
- Add `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`
- Add CI checks for critical examples (including `custom_node_plugins`)
- Add a small OS matrix (at least Linux + macOS; Windows if feasible)

## Motivation
CI should be the source of truth for contributor checks, not a partial subset of `CONTRIBUTING.md`.

## Definition of done
- One primary CI workflow covers PR + main validation
- Lint failures fail CI
- Example drift is caught automatically
- Cross-platform breakage is surfaced before merge

title: [PLG-01] Define external plugin packaging and compatibility rules
body:
## Description

The new runtime plugin system supports in-process registration, but it does not yet define how third-party plugins should be packaged, versioned, or compatibility-checked.

## Scope
- Define a plugin authoring/package contract for 0.1.x
- Document API compatibility expectations for plugins across runtime upgrades
- Define registry metadata/capability descriptors if needed
- Document safety boundaries and what remains in-process / unsandboxed

## Motivation
Without a compatibility contract, plugin support is useful for app-local composition but not yet a stable ecosystem surface.

## Definition of done
- Plugin authoring guidance exists for external users
- Compatibility rules are explicit
- At least one non-trivial packaged plugin example or reference layout exists

title: [OSS-03] Expand ecosystem integration examples beyond Axum
body:
## Description

The project now has better starter templates, but the concrete integration examples are still narrow (primarily Axum plus a small set of templates).

## Scope
- Add at least two more integration paths (for example: `tonic`, `actix-web`, pure Tokio worker, deployment-oriented examples)
- Document when to choose each integration path
- Ensure new examples compile in CI or a dedicated example validation job
- Update the examples index and onboarding docs

## Motivation
Adoption improves when users can map Oris onto the Rust stack they already run.

## Definition of done
- New ecosystem examples/guides are added
- Example selection guidance is documented
- The examples index clearly points users to the new paths

title: [PERF-01] Add a dedicated benchmark suite and regression policy
body:
## Description

Current performance coverage relies on stress-style tests inside the runtime test suite. There is no dedicated benchmark harness, stable baseline, or explicit regression policy.

## Scope
- Add repeatable benchmarks for key hot paths (dispatch, lease heartbeat, replay, API endpoints)
- Separate benchmark reporting from correctness tests
- Capture baseline outputs in a stable format
- Define how benchmark regressions are reviewed (CI threshold, manual gate, or scheduled run)

## Motivation
Stress tests are useful for correctness, but they are not a substitute for measured performance tracking.

## Definition of done
- A benchmark harness exists and is documented
- Baseline metrics are captured in-repo or artifacted consistently
- A clear regression review policy is defined

title: [API-01] Publish a machine-readable runtime API contract
body:
## Description

The runtime API is documented in `README.md`, but there is no machine-readable contract (OpenAPI / JSON schema) for operators, SDKs, or generated clients.

## Scope
- Generate an OpenAPI spec or equivalent machine-readable schema for the runtime API
- Cover the current v1 endpoints and request/response DTOs
- Publish the artifact in-repo (or expose it from the execution server)
- Add a drift check so docs/spec stay aligned

## Motivation
Text-only API docs do not scale well once the control-plane surface grows.

## Definition of done
- A machine-readable runtime API contract exists
- The spec is reproducible from code or checked in with drift protection
- README points to the canonical contract location

title: [SCH-07] Harden remaining scheduler and lease semantics
body:
## Description

The runtime repository, scheduler, and lease manager still contain explicit TODOs around transactional dispatch semantics, fairness, heartbeat grace handling, and split-brain protection.

## Scope
- Eliminate the remaining `phase1.1` scheduler/lease TODOs
- Define atomic attempt-status + lease-write semantics
- Use lease versions and heartbeat grace for stricter expiry handling
- Add tests for split-brain, fairness/ordering, and conflict scenarios

## Motivation
The core queue/lease path should no longer rely on skeleton semantics now that the v1 runtime surface has expanded.

## Definition of done
- Remaining dispatch-semantics TODOs in repository/scheduler/lease are removed
- Attempt ownership transitions are explicit and test-covered
- Split-brain and fairness regressions have dedicated tests

title: [SEC-07] Add supply-chain and dependency scanning to CI
body:
## Description

Current CI validates runtime security regressions, but it does not check dependency advisories, license policy, or supply-chain drift.

## Scope
- Add a dependency scanning job (`cargo audit`, `cargo deny`, or equivalent)
- Fail CI on unapproved advisories / license violations
- Commit the baseline config and document how exceptions are reviewed
- Keep the new job separate from runtime auth regression tests

## Motivation
Runtime API security tests do not protect against vulnerable or policy-breaking dependencies.

## Definition of done
- CI includes a supply-chain scan job
- Baseline config is versioned in the repository
- Exceptions are explicit and reviewable
- Failing advisories block merges by default

title: [REL-02] Fix crate metadata and repository link hygiene
body:
## Description

The crate manifest and docs still contain stale repository links from the previous GitHub location, and the crate does not declare a `rust-version`.

## Scope
- Update `repository` and `homepage` in `crates/oris-runtime/Cargo.toml`
- Update stale GitHub links in `README.md` and onboarding docs
- Declare a supported `rust-version`
- Add a lightweight validation so release metadata and public links stay consistent

## Motivation
Incorrect public metadata creates avoidable friction for users, package consumers, and release automation.

## Definition of done
- No stale GitHub URLs remain in release-facing docs/metadata
- `rust-version` is declared in the crate manifest
- Release metadata consistency is documented and/or checked automatically

title: [K1] Implement ExecutionStep Contract Freeze
body:
**Description:** Eliminate async side effects, hidden runtime mutations, and adapter nondeterminism by defining a strict, canonical execution contract.

**Tasks:**
- Implement the `ExecutionStep` trait (`execute(state, input) -> StepResult`).
- Enforce pure boundary conditions.
- Define and validate explicit inputs and outputs.

**Acceptance Criteria:** Adapters can only interact through the frozen step contract.

title: [K1] Implement Runtime Effect Capture
body:
**Description:** Intercept and record all side effects generated during execution.

**Tasks:**
- Introduce `RuntimeEffect` enum (`LLMCall`, `ToolCall`, `StateWrite`, `InterruptRaise`).
- Implement kernel-level hooks to log all instances of `RuntimeEffect` to the active thread context.

**Acceptance Criteria:** Zero uncaptured side effects leak into the execution state.

title: [K1] Build Determinism Guard & Execution Modes
body:
**Description:** Prevent nondeterministic operations from corrupting the state and introduce strict runtime modes.

**Tasks:**
- Implement `KernelMode` (`Normal`, `Record`, `Replay`, `Verify`).
- Add trap handlers to immediately fail execution on clock access, hardware randomness detection, or uncontrolled thread spawning.

**Acceptance Criteria:** Same run guarantees an identical event stream hash; replay mismatches are instantly detected and halted.

title: [K2] Build Canonical Execution Log Store
body:
**Description:** Move away from checkpoint-driven state management to an event-sourced log as the source of truth.

**Tasks:**
- Implement `ExecutionLog` struct (`thread_id`, `step_id`, `event_index`, `event`, `state_hash`).
- Refactor current checkpointing to act strictly as an optimization/snapshot layer.

title: [K2] Develop Replay Cursor Engine
body:
**Description:** Build the core algorithm to reconstruct state from historical events without triggering live side effects.

**Tasks:**
- Implement replay loop: Load checkpoint → Replay events → Inject recorded outputs → Reconstruct state.
- Ensure live tool execution is hard-disabled during replay.

title: [K2] Implement Replay Verification API
body:
**Description:** Create a diagnostic API to cryptographically verify the integrity of a run.

**Tasks:**
- Expose `oris kernel verify <thread_id>` CLI/API command.
- Implement validation logic for state hash equality, tool checksum matching, and interrupt consistency.

title: [K2] Enable Branch Replay (Timeline Forking)
body:
**Description:** Allow execution timelines to fork from a specific historical checkpoint.

**Tasks:**
- Implement logic to resume replay from checkpoint N, inject an alternate decision, and fork the event stream.

**Acceptance Criteria:** Reasoning timelines can be successfully reconstructed, audited, simulated, and forked.

title: [K3] Define and Store Kernel Interrupt Object
body:
**Description:** Standardize how interrupts are represented and persisted.

**Tasks:**
- Implement `Interrupt` struct (`id`, `thread_id`, `kind`, `payload_schema`, `created_at`).
- Ensure interrupts are flushed and stored alongside execution checkpoints.

title: [K3] Implement Execution Suspension State Machine
body:
**Description:** Handle the safe teardown of workers when an execution is paused.

**Tasks:**
- Implement kernel state transitions: `Running` → `Suspended` → `WaitingInput`.
- Ensure the worker safely exits and releases resources upon suspension.

title: [K3] Enforce Replay-Based Resume Semantics
body:
**Description:** Guarantee that resuming a suspended state does not rely on active memory.

**Tasks:**
- Implement resume logic strictly as: Replay + Inject Decision.
- Write tests to guarantee resuming N times yields identical results (idempotent resumes).

title: [K3] Build Unified Interrupt Routing Layer
body:
**Description:** Create a single resolver for all external interrupt sources.

**Tasks:**
- Implement `InterruptResolver` trait (`async fn resolve(interrupt) -> Value`).
- Map inputs from UI, agents, policy engines, and APIs through the resolver.

**Acceptance Criteria:** A process can be suspended, memory completely cleared, and successfully resumed days later.

title: [K4] Define and Enforce Plugin Categories
body:
**Description:** Standardize the types of plugins the kernel will recognize and load.

**Tasks:**
- Implement interfaces for `NodePlugin`, `ToolPlugin`, `MemoryPlugin`, `LLMAdapter`, and `SchedulerPlugin`.

title: [K4] Implement Plugin Determinism Declarations
body:
**Description:** Force plugins to declare their behavioral boundaries to the kernel.

**Tasks:**
- Require `PluginMetadata` (`deterministic`, `side_effects`, `replay_safe`) for all plugins.
- Build kernel enforcement logic (replay substitution, sandbox routing).

title: [K4] Build Plugin Execution Sandbox
body:
**Description:** Isolate plugin execution based on safety requirements.

**Tasks:**
- Implement execution mode routers (`InProcess`, `IsolatedProcess`, `Remote`).
- (Spike/Future): Blueprint WASM execution mode.

title: [K4] Implement Plugin Version Negotiation & Dynamic Registry
body:
**Description:** Prevent runtime corruption through strict validation and enable hot-loading.

**Tasks:**
- Add validation for `plugin_api_version`, `kernel_compat`, and `schema_hash` on load.
- Upgrade `NodePluginRegistry` to support dynamic hot-loading and unloading of validated plugins.

**Acceptance Criteria:** Third-party tools can be loaded and executed without threatening kernel determinism.

title: [K5] Finalize Lease-Based Execution
body:
**Description:** Formalize ownership of execution attempts.

**Tasks:**
- Implement `WorkerLease` to strictly enforce single-owner execution.
- Build lease expiry, recovery, and replay-restart logic.

title: [K5] Implement Zero-Data-Loss Failure Recovery Loop
body:
**Description:** Automate the recovery pipeline when a worker abruptly crashes.

**Tasks:**
- Implement the crash recovery pipeline: Lease expires → Checkpoint reloads → Execution replays → Dispatched to new worker.

title: [K5] Build Context-Aware Scheduler Kernel
body:
**Description:** Upgrade the dispatcher to route tasks intelligently across the cluster.

**Tasks:**
- Inject awareness of tenant limits, priority queues, plugin requirements, interrupt backlogs, and specific worker capabilities into the scheduling algorithm.

title: [K5] Implement Safe Backpressure Engine & Kernel Observability
body:
**Description:** Protect the cluster from overload and provide deep kernel insights (not just standard logs).

**Tasks:**
- Build safe rejection mechanisms returning explicit `tenant_limit` reasons.
- Expose telemetry for Reasoning Timeline, Lease Graph, Replay Cost, and Interrupt Latency.

**Acceptance Criteria:** `kill -9` on an active worker → cluster restarts → resumes exactly where it left off with an identical reasoning outcome.

title: EvoKernel Wave 0: Add experimental crate skeletons and feature wiring
body:
## Summary
Establish the repo topology for the full EvoKernel blueprint without changing stable runtime paths.

## Tasks
- Add new workspace members for `oris-governor`, `oris-evolution-network`, `oris-economics`, `oris-spec`, and `oris-agent-contract`
- Split `oris-evolution`, `oris-evokernel`, and `oris-sandbox` internals into modules while preserving current behavior
- Add experimental features in `oris-runtime`: `governor-experimental`, `evolution-network-experimental`, `economics-experimental`, `spec-experimental`, `agent-contract-experimental`, and `full-evolution-experimental`
- Wire `full-evolution-experimental` to aggregate all experimental evolution layers
- Keep all new crates `publish = false`
- Preserve existing `oris_runtime::evolution` imports and stable module paths

## Acceptance Criteria
- Workspace compiles
- `examples/evo_oris_repo` still compiles without import path changes
- Existing `oris_runtime::evolution` API remains usable

title: EvoKernel Wave 1: Add sqlite kernel stores and unify kernel trace/timeline
body:
## Summary
Make the kernel locally durable across in-memory, SQLite, and Postgres, and expose richer execution observability.

## Tasks
- Implement `SqliteEventStore` and `SqliteSnapshotStore` in `oris-kernel`
- Make `sqlite-persistence` in `oris-kernel` a real feature instead of a placeholder
- Add a unified `KernelTraceEvent` model with `run_id`, `seq`, `step_id`, `action_id`, `kind`, and `timestamp_ms`
- Update `execution_log.rs` and `timeline.rs` to produce consistent event fields
- Populate `KernelObservability` in `oris-execution-runtime` with real values instead of placeholders
- Thread richer trace context through existing execution server response models without breaking routes

## Acceptance Criteria
- Kernel replay tests pass for in-memory, SQLite, and Postgres stores
- Each `KernelObservability` field is populated on at least one execution path
- Execution server API only gains optional fields; no breaking removals

title: EvoKernel Wave 3: Introduce governor crate and governor-aware capture flow
body:
## Summary
Add an append-only, policy-only Governor layer that decides promotion, cooldown, and revocation without becoming a second source of truth.

## Tasks
- Create `oris-governor` with `GovernorConfig`, `GovernorInput`, `GovernorDecision`, `CoolingWindow`, `RevocationReason`, and `CandidateSource`
- Define `Governor` trait and a default policy implementation
- Default policy: first local success => `Candidate`
- Promote only after `success_count >= 3` and blast radius within limits
- Keep high-blast-radius mutations as `Candidate`
- Apply a default 30 minute cooldown after promotion
- Auto-revoke after two replay validation failures
- Add `capture_mutation_with_governor(...) -> CaptureOutcome` to `oris-evokernel`
- Preserve `capture_successful_mutation(...) -> Capsule` as a compatibility wrapper
- Ensure replay only attempts `Promoted` assets by default

## Acceptance Criteria
- Repeated success transitions a candidate into a promoted asset
- High blast radius never auto-promotes
- Revoked assets stop being selected immediately

title: EvoKernel Wave 4: Implement OEN protocol and quarantine import pipeline
body:
## Summary
Add a minimal Oris Evolution Network protocol layer and the local quarantine pipeline for remote assets.

## Tasks
- Create `oris-evolution-network` for protocol DTOs and canonical hashing
- Add `EvolutionEnvelope`, `MessageType`, `NetworkAsset`, `PublishRequest`, `FetchQuery`, `FetchResponse`, and `RevokeNotice`
- Fix envelope defaults: `protocol = "oen"`, `protocol_version = "0.1"`
- Compute `content_hash` from canonical JSON without the `content_hash` field
- Add `export_promoted_assets(...) -> EvolutionEnvelope` in `oris-evokernel`
- Add `import_remote_envelope(...) -> ImportOutcome` in `oris-evokernel`
- On import: validate hash, validate schema, append remote import and quarantine events, run local validation, then hand off to governor
- Add feature-gated experimental routes for publish, fetch, and revoke in runtime/server facades

## Acceptance Criteria
- A promoted local capsule can be exported as a valid envelope
- Invalid hash or schema is rejected with audit-visible events
- Remote assets remain `Quarantined` until local validation completes

title: EvoKernel Wave 2: Add asset lifecycle states and evolution event extensions
body:
## Summary
Upgrade the evolution domain from immediate commit-only behavior to explicit lifecycle-managed assets.

## Tasks
- Add `AssetState` with `Candidate`, `Promoted`, `Revoked`, `Archived`, and `Quarantined`
- Extend `Gene` and `Capsule` with `state`
- Extend `Outcome` with `lines_changed` and `replay_verified`
- Add `BlastRadius` support sourced from patch text in `oris-sandbox`
- Extend `EvolutionEvent` with promotion, revocation, quarantine, remote import, and spec-link variants
- Update projection rebuild logic so selector only uses `Promoted` assets by default
- Keep old log compatibility by defaulting legacy records to `Promoted`

## Acceptance Criteria
- Successful mutation capture records assets in explicit lifecycle states
- Projection rebuild reflects event-chain-derived state accurately
- Existing historical `events.jsonl` remains readable

title: EvoKernel Wave 7: Add agent runtime contract and DEVLOOP end-to-end example
body:
## Summary
Introduce a stable agent-facing runtime contract and prove the full DEVLOOP path with an end-to-end example.

## Tasks
- Create `oris-agent-contract`
- Add `AgentTask`, `MutationProposal`, `ExecutionFeedback`, `AgentCapabilityLevel`, and `ProposalTarget`
- Enforce that agents only submit proposals while kernel owns mutation execution and evolution persistence
- Add `capture_from_proposal(...)` and `feedback_for_agent(...)` in `oris-evokernel`
- Extend `examples/evo_oris_repo` to cover `task -> proposal -> mutation -> validation -> candidate -> promoted -> replay`
- Document feature flags and maturity status in `docs/evokernel/`

## Acceptance Criteria
- At least two agent sources can enter the same capture pipeline
- A recurring task can replay on the second pass without repeating agent reasoning
- The end-to-end example runs under `full-evolution-experimental`

title: EvoKernel Wave 6: Add OUSL YAML spec compiler and spec-linked mutations
body:
## Summary
Introduce the spec layer so repository-native YAML specs compile into mutations and remain traceable through evolution events.

## Tasks
- Create `oris-spec`
- Make OUSL v0.1 YAML the only accepted first-pass spec format
- Add `SpecDocument`, `SpecId`, `SpecVersion`, `SpecConstraint`, `CompiledMutationPlan`, and `SpecCompiler`
- Standardize spec directories under `/specs/behavior`, `/specs/repair`, `/specs/optimization`, and `/specs/evolution`
- Extend `MutationIntent` with `spec_id: Option<String>`
- Add `prepare_mutation_from_spec(...)` to `oris-evokernel`
- Append `SpecLinked` evolution events for spec-driven mutations
- Allow selector to optionally narrow by `spec_id` while still defaulting to signals first

## Acceptance Criteria
- A minimal YAML spec compiles into a `PreparedMutation`
- Invalid specs fail before sandbox execution
- Evolution logs can trace a capsule back to its originating `spec_id`

title: EvoKernel Wave 5: Add EVU ledger and reputation-based economics layer
body:
## Summary
Introduce a non-financial local economics layer for publish gating, validator incentives, and reputation weighting.

## Tasks
- Create `oris-economics`
- Add `EvuLedger`, `EvuAccount`, `StakePolicy`, `ReputationRecord`, and `ValidationSettlement`
- Require stake before publish of remote-facing assets
- Increase publisher reputation and EVU on successful remote reuse
- Burn stake or reduce reputation on failed remote reuse
- Penalize validator reputation when validator reports diverge from local final validation
- Expose economics outputs as governor/selector inputs without replacing validation truth

## Acceptance Criteria
- Insufficient EVU blocks publish but does not block local replay
- Remote reuse success/failure updates the ledger
- Reputation can be used as an optional selector weight without overriding validation truth

title: EvoKernel Wave 1.5: Deterministic Execution Core
body:
## Summary

Implement deterministic execution core for Phase 1.5, extending Wave 1 to add step-by-step execution tracking, retry safety, interrupt recovery, and replay engine v0.

## Context

Phase 1 (Week 2-3) covers deterministic execution per [implementation-roadmap.md](docs/evokernel/implementation-roadmap.md). Wave 1 (#64) covers SQLite stores and trace/timeline, but detailed execution components are missing.

## Implementation Details

### Step-by-Step Execution Tracking
- Record each execution step with input state, mutation applied, output, and environment hash
- Implement deterministic execution hash computation
- Add step-level checkpointing for granular replay

### Retry Safety
- Ensure identical execution results across retries
- Track retry count and ensure idempotency
- Implement deterministic mutation application

### Interrupt Recovery
- Save execution state on interrupt (SIGINT, crash)
- Resume from last checkpoint
- Maintain execution determinism after recovery

### Replay Engine v0
- Implement deterministic replay from captured trace
- Verify identical output reproduction
- Support environment fingerprint matching

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Step-level execution trace persisted to SQLite
- [ ] Retry produces identical output (deterministic hash matches)
- [ ] Interrupted execution can resume from last checkpoint
- [ ] Replay engine reproduces identical execution results
- [ ] Deterministic execution hash validates correctly

## References

- [kernel.md - Execution Engine](docs/evokernel/kernel.md)
- [implementation-roadmap.md - Phase 1](docs/evokernel/implementation-roadmap.md)

---
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: EvoKernel Wave 2.1: Evolution Solidification Engine
body:
## Summary

Implement evolution solidification engine for Phase 2, extending Wave 2 to add Codex adapter, signal extraction, Solidifier implementation, and append-only evolution store.

## Context

Phase 2 (Week 4-5) covers evolution solidification per [implementation-roadmap.md](docs/evokernel/implementation-roadmap.md). Wave 2 (#67) covers asset lifecycle states and events, but detailed solidification components are missing.

## Implementation Details

### Codex Adapter
- Capture patch diff from code changes
- Collect execution logs and validation results
- Store environment fingerprint alongside artifacts

### Signal Extraction from Logs
- Extract deterministic signals from:
  - Compiler diagnostics
  - Stack traces
  - Execution logs
  - Failure signatures
  - Performance telemetry
- Implement signal normalization and hashing

### Solidifier Engine
- Implement Gene creation (strategy summary, constraints, validation rules)
- Implement Capsule creation (gene reference, diff hash, confidence score, environment fingerprint, outcome metrics)
- Emit EvolutionEvent for immutable lineage tracking
- Integrate with append-only evolution store

### Append-Only Evolution Store
- Implement immutable storage for Gene, Capsule, EvolutionEvent
- Support query by signal, gene, environment fingerprint
- Ensure auditability and replay reconstruction

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Codex adapter captures patch diff, logs, validation result
- [ ] Signal extraction produces deterministic signal hash
- [ ] Successful executions automatically generate Capsule
- [ ] Evolution store maintains immutable history
- [ ] Gene can be queried by signal pattern

## References

- [evolution.md - Evolution Assets](docs/evokernel/evolution.md)
- [implementation-roadmap.md - Phase 2](docs/evokernel/implementation-roadmap.md)

---
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: EvoKernel Wave 3: Selection and Replay System
body:
## Summary

Implement selection and replay system for Phase 3, enabling self-reuse of proven solutions and reducing reasoning frequency.

## Context

Phase 3 (Week 6-7) covers selection and replay per [implementation-roadmap.md](docs/evokernel/implementation-roadmap.md). This is currently missing a dedicated issue.

## Implementation Details

### Selector Implementation
- Implement selection factors:
  - Success rate (historical success percentage)
  - Reuse count (how often selected)
  - Recency (last used timestamp)
  - Environment match (environment fingerprint similarity)
- Implement ranking algorithm combining all factors

### Replay Executor
- Detect signals from current execution context
- Find matching Capsule by signal pattern
- Apply stored patch/diff to execution
- Validate result matches expected outcome

### Signal Detection System
- Monitor runtime for signal patterns
- Match against known gene signals
- Trigger selection when confidence threshold met

### Environment Fingerprint Matching
- Capture environment characteristics (OS, versions, dependencies)
- Calculate similarity score between execution environments
- Weight selection by environment match quality

### Capsule Lookup and Ranking
- Query evolution store by signal
- Rank candidates by composite score
- Select best match above threshold

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Repeated issues solved without new reasoning
- [ ] Token usage decreases for known patterns
- [ ] Selector ranks candidates by composite score
- [ ] Replay successfully applies stored Capsule
- [ ] Environment fingerprint matching improves selection accuracy

## References

- [evolution.md - Selection and Replay](docs/evokernel/evolution.md)
- [implementation-roadmap.md - Phase 3](docs/evokernel/implementation-roadmap.md)

title: EvoKernel Wave 8: Observability and Metrics
body:
## Summary

Implement observability and metrics collection system to track evolution performance, success rates, and system health.

## Context

Per [implementation-roadmap.md](docs/evokernel/implementation-roadmap.md) Section 10 - Parallel Workstreams, observability is a critical parallel workstream that supports all phases.

## Implementation Details

### Core Metrics

Implement collection for:
- **Replay Success Rate**: Percentage of replay attempts that successfully reproduce expected outcomes
- **Promotion Ratio**: Rate of Capsule promotion from Candidate to Promoted
- **Revoke Frequency**: How often strategies are revoked due to regression
- **Mutation Velocity**: Rate of new mutations per time window

### Metrics Pipeline

- Add metrics collection points in:
  - Execution engine (latency, step count)
  - Solidifier (genes/capsules generated)
  - Selector (selection count, match quality)
  - Governor (mutations applied, revocations)
- Implement metrics aggregation and storage
- Add Prometheus/OTLP export capability

### Dashboard Integration

- Expose metrics via HTTP endpoint
- Support Grafana dashboard consumption
- Implement health check endpoint

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Replay success rate tracked and exposed
- [ ] Promotion ratio metrics collected
- [ ] Revoke frequency tracked over time
- [ ] Mutation velocity metrics available
- [ ] Metrics endpoint serves Prometheus format

## References

- [implementation-roadmap.md - Observability](docs/evokernel/implementation-roadmap.md)

title: EvoKernel Wave 9: Evolution Testing Suite
body:
## Summary

Implement comprehensive testing infrastructure for deterministic replay, sandbox safety, and governor regression detection.

## Context

Per [implementation-roadmap.md](docs/evokernel/implementation-roadmap.md) Section 10 - Parallel Workstreams, testing infrastructure is critical for evolution safety and reliability.

## Implementation Details

### Deterministic Replay Tests

- Create test suite that verifies identical execution reproduction
- Test across
 different environments- Test across different time windows
- Verify deterministic hash consistency

### Sandbox Safety Tests

- Test mutation isolation boundaries
- Verify unauthorized code cannot escape sandbox
- Test resource limits (memory, CPU, network)
- Verify cleanup on termination

### Governor Regression Tests

- Test mutation rate limit enforcement
- Test blast radius calculation accuracy
- Test confidence decay function
- Test cooling window behavior
- Verify regression detection triggers correctly

### Integration Tests

- End-to-end evolution loop tests
- Selection -> Mutation -> Execution -> Validation -> Solidification
- Multi-run stability tests

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Deterministic replay tests pass consistently
- [ ] Sandbox safety tests verify isolation
- [ ] Governor regression tests cover all policies
- [ ] Integration tests verify full evolution loop

title: EvoKernel Wave 10: Governor Detailed Policies
body:
## Summary

Implement detailed governor policies extending Wave 3 (#65) - mutation rate limits, blast radius calculation, confidence decay, regression detection, and cooling window management.

## Context

Wave 3 (#65) introduces the governor crate and governor-aware capture flow. This issue covers the detailed policy implementations needed for safe evolution.

## Implementation Details

### Mutation Rate Limit Enforcement
- Implement configurable limits:
  - Max mutations per time window
  - Max concurrent mutations
  - Per-capsule mutation limits
- Add rate limiting middleware in execution path

### Blast Radius Calculation
- Implement code change impact analysis
- Calculate affected function/module count
- Measure dependency distance
- Set blast radius thresholds

### Confidence Decay Implementation
- Implement confidence score calculation:
  - Initial confidence from validation score
  - Time-based decay function
  - Success/failure adjustment
- Persist confidence history

### Regression Detection System
- Monitor success rate trends
- Detect significant drops below threshold
- Trigger automatic revocation of degraded strategies
- Implement rollback capability

### Cooling Window Management
- Implement cooling period after:
  - Failed promotion attempts
  - Revocation events
  - High mutation activity
- Configure cooling window durations

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Mutation rate limits enforced correctly
- [ ] Blast radius calculated and capped
- [ ] Confidence decays over time per spec
- [ ] Regression detection triggers auto-revoke
- [ ] Cooling windows prevent rapid re-attempts

## References

- [governor.md](docs/evokernel/governor.md)
- [implementation-roadmap.md - Phase 4](docs/evokernel/implementation-roadmap.md)

- Event-driven coordination
- Timeout and retry handling

## Acceptance Criteria

title: EvoKernel Wave 12: Bootstrap and Initial Seeding
body:
## Summary

Implement bootstrap mechanism and initial gene seeding for the evolution system, enabling self-starting capability without pre-existing knowledge.

## Context

Per [devloop.md](docs/evokernel/devloop.md) Section 14, bootstrap interaction and first genes should be generated automatically. This is the final piece for a fully self-contained evolution system.

## Implementation Details

### Initial Seeding Strategy

- Implement automatic seed gene generation:
  - Common patterns from standard library
  - Basic algorithm implementations
  - Fundamental data structures
- Add curated seed capsules for common problem types

### Bootstrap Execution

- Implement first-run bootstrap flow:
  - Detect empty evolution store
  - Apply seeding strategy
  - Verify seed genes are valid

### Seed Validation

- Validate seed genes:
  - Syntax correctness
  - Basic functionality tests
  - Environment compatibility

### Self-Generation Capability

- Implement mechanism for system to:
  - Generate new genes from execution
  - Improve existing genes over time
  - Reach critical mass for self-improvement

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] System starts with non-empty gene store
- [ ] Initial seeds cover common patterns
- [ ] Bootstrap flow works on first run
- [ ] System generates new genes automatically
- [ ] Seeds improve over time through evolution

title: EvoKernel Wave 14: Lifecycle Audit Follow-up
body:
## Summary

Continue the post-documentation EvoKernel code audit as a dedicated follow-up issue, focusing on lifecycle state transitions, remote import semantics, replay attribution, and projection/metrics consistency.

## Context

The original documentation sync issue `#79` is closed, but the audit expanded into runtime behavior verification and surfaced several implementation/documentation drift items. Some of those have already been fixed locally during the current review pass, and the remaining audit work should be tracked independently.

## Current Status

Already fixed during the active audit pass:
- Replay reuse attribution now records the current replay execution `run_id` via `replay_or_fallback_for_run(...)`.
- Local governor capture now uses existing confidence context and emits `GeneRevoked` for local revocations.
- Public replay candidate previews no longer expose bootstrap-only candidates that replay cannot execute.
- Remote import now records publisher attribution per successfully imported asset, preserving sender ownership across partial import failures.
- Remote genes now enter `Quarantined` on import and only promote after the first successful local replay.

## Remaining Scope

- Audit EvoKernel event sequences for any remaining lifecycle state drift across local capture, replay, revoke, and remote import paths.
- Verify projection rebuild semantics stay consistent with emitted event order.
- Check metrics snapshots and exported assets for any undercounting or state leakage.
- Reconcile any remaining Evo docs that still overstate or understate the shipped runtime behavior.
- Run broader validation once the audit fixes settle; current coverage is targeted regression testing only.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Remaining EvoKernel lifecycle inconsistencies are identified and either fixed or explicitly documented.
- [ ] Event log semantics are consistent for equivalent state transitions.
- [ ] Projection, metrics, and export behavior match the intended lifecycle state model.
- [ ] Broader validation scope is defined or executed before closeout.

## References

- Follow-up from `#79`
- Current local audit pass on `crates/oris-evokernel/src/core.rs`

title: Follow up remote replay scoring and export scan cost
body:
## Summary

Track the two non-blocking issues found during the self-review of PR #83 / the v0.13.1 release.

## Scope

1. Normalize remote cold-start replay scoring.
   - `quarantined_remote_exact_match_candidates(...)` now counts matched gene signals and divides by the number of requested signals.
   - That can produce scores greater than `1.0` when a single query term matches multiple gene signals, which biases ranking toward redundant overlapping signals instead of better query coverage.

2. Reduce duplicate event-log scans in export and fetch paths.
   - `export_promoted_assets_from_store(...)` and `fetch_assets_from_store(...)` already rebuild the projection.
   - The new replay export helper then calls `scan(1)` again to recover `MutationDeclared` / `SpecLinked`, which adds an extra full event-log read on hot paths.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Remote cold-start candidate scoring is bounded and reflects distinct query coverage, not duplicate overlapping signal strings.
- A regression test covers the ranking behavior so a single query term cannot inflate the score above the intended range.
- Export / fetch avoid the extra full event-log pass, or otherwise derive the replay payloads from data already available in the same read path.
- Targeted validation covers the updated replay selection and asset export behavior.

## References

- Follow-up from PR #83
- Released context: `oris-evolution v0.2.1`, `oris-evokernel v0.6.1`, `oris-runtime v0.13.1`

title: [EVO] Track staged self-evolution from replay baseline to supervised devloop
body:
## Why
We now have a checked-in self-evolution acceptance baseline and regression suite, but the current implementation is still constrained replay-driven reuse, not a closed-loop autonomous devloop.

## Current baseline
- capture verified mutations into the evolution store
- replay learned tasks on later runs
- keep remote assets quarantined until local validation
- preserve replay behavior across restart with the same store
- keep false-positive replay under control in the current regression suite

## Next stages
1. Expand task-class generalization beyond current normalized signal matching.
2. Add continuous confidence control: decay, revalidation, demotion, and freshness-aware ranking.
3. Close the agent feedback loop with structured replay hints, fallback reasons, and reasoning-avoidance metrics.
4. Move toward supervised DEVLOOP orchestration for bounded development tasks.
5. Harden federated evolution: attribution fidelity, economics, revocation propagation, and network consistency.

## Deliverables
- break this direction into issue-sized follow-up work
- keep the self-evolution acceptance checklist aligned with shipped behavior
- keep the replay regression suite as the minimum test gate

## References
- docs/evokernel/self-evolution-acceptance-checklist.md
- docs/evokernel/implementation-roadmap.md

title: [EVO-01] Expand deterministic task-class replay matching beyond normalized signals
body:
Parent: #86

## Goal
Move from exact or currently normalized signal replay into broader deterministic task-class reuse without increasing false positives.

## Scope
- enrich deterministic signal extraction from intent, diff, validation logs, and stage outputs
- add a broader task-class replay matching layer beyond current exact and normalized matches
- keep negative controls proving unrelated tasks do not replay
- update the self-evolution acceptance checklist when the new boundary ships

## Definition of done
- at least one learned mutation replays across multiple semantically adjacent signal variants
- false-positive replay remains controlled in the black-box regression suite
- replay ranking distinguishes near-match from true no-match more clearly than today

## Non-goals
- embeddings or LLM-only similarity matching
- autonomous planning or orchestration

## Validation
- targeted new regression coverage
- cargo test -p oris-evokernel --test evolution_lifecycle_regression

title: [EVO-02] Add a continuous confidence lifecycle for evolution assets
body:
Parent: #86

## Goal
Move from one-shot promotion into continuously managed asset trust with decay, revalidation, and demotion paths.

## Scope
- add confidence decay over time
- define revalidation or shadow-check triggers
- support automatic demotion or revocation on drift
- make replay ranking freshness-aware
- expose confidence-driven transitions in the acceptance checklist and metrics surface

## Definition of done
- stale or environment-diverged assets lose priority automatically
- at least one regression path proves decay or drift changes replay eligibility
- confidence-driven state transitions are visible and auditable

## Non-goals
- full autonomous background scheduling across the whole runtime
- non-deterministic heuristic revocation

## Validation
- targeted unit and regression coverage for decay, revalidation, and demotion
- cargo test -p oris-evolution --lib
- cargo test -p oris-evokernel --test evolution_lifecycle_regression

title: [EVO-03] Close the replay feedback loop for agent callers
body:
Parent: #86

## Goal
Make replay outcomes first-class agent-facing signals so repeated work measurably reduces reasoning and implementation effort.

## Scope
- expose structured replay hints for callers
- expose explicit fallback reasons that a planner can consume
- add metrics for replay utilization and reasoning avoided per task class
- wire the current proposal path and example flow to consume replay feedback
- update docs and examples for the new feedback boundary

## Definition of done
- the agent-facing API surfaces actionable replay result data, not just a binary outcome
- repeated tasks show lower reasoning dependence in the test harness or example flow
- fallback paths remain explicit and auditable

## Non-goals
- autonomous issue intake
- autonomous release or PR orchestration

## Validation
- targeted integration coverage for replay feedback
- cargo test -p oris-runtime --test evolution_feature_wiring --features full-evolution-experimental
- cargo test -p oris-evokernel --test evolution_lifecycle_regression

title: [EVO-04] Introduce supervised DEVLOOP orchestration for bounded tasks
body:
Parent: #86

## Goal
Move from reusable replay memory into a supervised, bounded development loop for a narrow class of tasks.

## Scope
- define a bounded task intake model
- add deterministic task classification or routing for the bounded scope
- define the gated mutation proposal and validation pipeline
- define explicit human approval boundaries
- keep the execution path auditable and policy-controlled

## Definition of done
- at least one bounded task class can run end-to-end through a supervised DEVLOOP
- failure paths remain gated and never bypass policy
- the flow stops short of autonomous release behavior

## Non-goals
- fully autonomous repository maintenance
- ungated branch, PR, or publish automation

## Validation
- bounded end-to-end DEVLOOP smoke coverage
- targeted policy and fallback regression coverage

title: [EVO-05] Harden federated evolution attribution, economics, and revocation
body:
Parent: #86

## Goal
Make multi-node evolution sharing trustworthy under attribution, economics, retries, restart, and revocation pressure.

## Scope
- harden publisher and validator attribution fidelity
- keep reputation weighting and settlement behavior consistent
- strengthen anti-spam publish economics
- define and validate revocation propagation
- keep import and export paths consistent under duplicate import, partial failure, and restart

## Definition of done
- remote reuse settlement remains attributable and stable under restart
- revocation propagates without corrupting local state
- distributed import and export paths remain idempotent and auditable under retries

## Non-goals
- multi-org product packaging
- non-local trust assumptions that bypass quarantine

## Validation
- targeted distributed regression coverage
- cargo test -p oris-evokernel --lib
- cargo test -p oris-evokernel --test evolution_lifecycle_regression
